macro_events.event_summary: Jamie Dimon publicly warns the next credit crisis will be worse than markets expect, amplifying concerns surfaced in big bank earnings about deteriorating credit quality.
primary_drivers: Rising credit loss provisions / NPL fears compress bank earnings
Risk-off rotation away from levered/sub-prime credit exposure
Flight to quality benefiting safe-haven assets and credit-quality monitors

beneficiaries.rationale: Moody's revenue rises with credit downgrade cycles and increased demand for credit risk analytics; ratings activity historically picks up during stress periods.
S&P Global ratings/risk services see higher engagement as credit dispersion widens; also benefits from defensive subscription revenue mix.

Counterintuitive beneficiary: Dimon's own bank is positioned as the 'fortress balance sheet' — in prior credit cycles JPM gained share and acquired distressed assets (Bear, WaMu, FRC). Relative outperformance vs. regional peers.

losers.rationale: Heavy auto loan exposure with rising sub-prime delinquencies; most credit-sensitive of the consumer lenders and trades as a high-beta proxy for consumer credit deterioration.
Capital One is dominated by credit card and sub-prime auto; charge-off rates already elevated and Dimon's warning amplifies provision-build fears.
Synchrony's private-label card book is concentrated in lower-FICO consumers — first to crack in a credit downturn; direct read-through from Dimon's consumer credit commentary.
Pure-play regional with outsized CRE concentration relative to capital; sentiment-driven selloffs in regionals whenever credit-crisis rhetoric escalates (echoes 2023 SVB pattern).
Blackstone's BDC — private credit / direct lending vehicles are at the center of the 'next credit crisis' narrative; mark-to-market and NAV concerns intensify on systemic warnings.
Largest publicly traded BDC; direct exposure to middle-market leveraged loans which are the specific area Dimon and other bank CEOs have flagged as opaque/risky.
Sallie Mae — consumer credit (private student loans) with rising delinquency trends; high-beta to any 'consumer is cracking' narrative coming from bank earnings.

headlines: Jamie Dimon says the next credit crisis will be 'worse than people expect' — here's what big bank earnings have revealed
